title: Work and Profession
description: The vocabulary and functional language of the Spanish-speaking workplace: jobs and the company (el empleo, la empresa, el sueldo, el horario, el contrato), the job hunt (el currículum, la entrevista, solicitar, contratar, despedir), describing your duties (encargarse de, ser responsable de) and getting things done at work (pedir un día libre, una reunión, un ascenso). Watch two classic false friends: éxito (success, not exit) and carrera (degree/career/race).

cards.notes: *El empleo* (employment, a job) and *el trabajo* (work, a job) overlap. *Buscar empleo* / *buscar trabajo* = to look for a job. *Estar en paro* / *estar desempleado* = to be unemployed.
*La empresa* is the everyday word for a company or firm. *El departamento* = the department; *la oficina* = the office. Note *empresa* is feminine: *una empresa grande*.
*El sueldo* and *el salario* both mean pay. *Cobrar* = to get paid / to earn (*cobro 2000 euros al mes*). *Un aumento de sueldo* = a pay rise.

cards.front: el horario / la jornada / el contrato
cards.back: the schedule / the working day / the contract
cards.notes: *El horario* = the (working) hours or schedule. *La jornada* = the working day: *jornada completa* (full-time), *media jornada* (part-time). *El contrato* = the contract (*un contrato fijo* = a permanent contract).

cards.front: el/la jefe/a · el/la compañero/a de trabajo
cards.notes: *El jefe / la jefa* = the boss. *El compañero / la compañera de trabajo* = a colleague. False-friend alert: a *colega* is more of a buddy in Spain; for a work colleague say *compañero/a*.

cards.front: el currículum · la entrevista de trabajo · la oferta de empleo
cards.back: the CV/résumé · the job interview · the job offer/posting
cards.notes: *El currículum* (CV) — full form *currículum vítae*. *La entrevista de trabajo* = job interview. *La oferta de empleo* = a job posting/vacancy. *Solicitar un puesto* = to apply for a position.

cards.back: to hire ↔ to fire / lay off
cards.notes: *Contratar a alguien* = to hire someone. *Despedir a alguien* = to fire/lay someone off (*despedir* is an -IR stem-changer: *despido, despides*...). *Solicitar* = to apply for / request.

cards.front: encargarse de · ser responsable de · tener experiencia en
cards.back: to be in charge of · to be responsible for · to have experience in
cards.notes: Describe your role: *Me encargo de las ventas* (I'm in charge of sales). *Soy responsable del equipo* (I'm responsible for the team). *Tengo experiencia en marketing* (I have experience in marketing).

cards.front: pedir un día libre · pedir un ascenso · convocar una reunión
cards.back: to ask for a day off · to ask for a promotion · to call a meeting
cards.notes: *Pedir un día libre* = ask for a day off. *Pedir un ascenso* = ask for a promotion. *Una reunión* = a meeting — and you **asistir a** a meeting (see the asistir card).

cards.back: to attend a meeting (asistir = to ATTEND, not to assist)
cards.notes: False-friend trap: *asistir a* means **to attend / be present at**, NOT to assist. *Asisto a la reunión* = I attend the meeting. To *assist/help* someone is *ayudar a alguien*. *Atender* (yet another) = to serve/see to a customer.
False friend: *el éxito* = **success**, never 'exit'. *Tener éxito* = to be successful; *una empresa de éxito* = a successful company. The way out / EXIT sign is **la salida**.

False friend: *la carrera* most often means a **university degree/major** (*estudié la carrera de Derecho* = I did a law degree), and also a **career** (*una carrera profesional*) or a **race** (*una carrera de coches*). Don't assume it only means 'career'.

steps.title: Talking about jobs and the workplace
steps.body:
# Work and Profession

This lesson gives you the everyday vocabulary of the Spanish-speaking workplace, plus the functional phrases you need to operate inside one.

## The job and the company

| Spanish | English |
|---|---|
| el empleo / el trabajo | the job / work |
| la empresa / la compañía | the company / firm |
| el sueldo / el salario | the salary / pay |
| el horario | the (working) hours / schedule |
| la jornada | the working day (*jornada completa* = full-time) |
| el contrato | the contract |
| el/la jefe/a | the boss |
| el/la compañero/a de trabajo | the colleague / co-worker |
| el departamento | the department |

> *Cobrar* = to get paid / earn; *un aumento de sueldo* = a pay rise; *un contrato fijo* = a permanent contract.

steps.title: The job hunt and describing what you do
steps.body:
## Looking for a job

- **el currículum** — the CV / résumé
- **la entrevista de trabajo** — the job interview
- **la oferta de empleo** — the job posting / vacancy
- **solicitar un puesto** — to apply for a position
- **contratar** — to hire  ·  **despedir** — to fire / lay off

## Describing your duties and skills

- **encargarse de** — to be in charge of: *Me encargo de las ventas.*
- **ser responsable de** — to be responsible for: *Soy responsable del equipo.*
- **tener experiencia en** — to have experience in: *Tengo experiencia en marketing.*

## Two false friends to lock in now

- **éxito** = **success**, NOT 'exit'. *Tener éxito* = to be successful. The way out is **la salida**.
- **carrera** = a university **degree/major**, a **career**, or a **race** — context decides. *Estudié la carrera de Medicina* = I studied medicine (a degree).

steps.title: Functional language and the subjunctive at work
steps.body:
## Getting things done at work

- **pedir un día libre** — to ask for a day off
- **pedir un ascenso** — to ask for a promotion
- **convocar / tener una reunión** — to call / have a meeting
- **asistir a una reunión** — to **attend** a meeting

> False-friend trap: **asistir a** = to **attend / be present at**, NOT to assist. To assist/help someone = *ayudar a alguien*.

## Wishes and not-yet-found jobs → subjunctive

When you describe a job you want but **haven't found yet**, the relative clause goes in the **subjunctive**, because the thing is hypothetical:

- *Busco un trabajo que **sea** interesante.* — I'm looking for a job that is interesting (any such job).
- *Quiero una empresa que **pague** bien.* — I want a company that pays well.

Compare a real, known job (indicative): *Tengo un trabajo que **es** interesante.*

And to talk about wishes politely, use the conditional: *Me **gustaría** trabajar en el extranjero.* (I would like to work abroad).

exercise.prompt: Match each Spanish word with its English meaning.

steps.title: A job you haven't found yet

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I'm looking for a job that is interesting.* (not-yet-found job → subjunctive: ser → sea)
accept: Busco un trabajo que sea interesante.
busco un trabajo que sea interesante
Busco un trabajo que sea interesante
Busco un empleo que sea interesante.
exercise.hint: Desired/unknown job → relative clause with subjunctive; ser → sea.
distractors: Busco un trabajo que es interesante.
Busco una salida que sea interesante.
Busco un trabajo que tenga éxito interesante.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I would like to work in a big company.* (conditional: gustar)
accept: Me gustaría trabajar en una empresa grande.
me gustaría trabajar en una empresa grande
Me gustaria trabajar en una empresa grande.
Me gustaría trabajar en una gran empresa.
Me gustaría trabajar en una empresa grande
distractors: Me gusta trabajar en una empresa grande.
Me gustaría asistir en una empresa grande.
Me gustaría trabajar en una salida grande.

exercise.prompt: Which sentence correctly says 'The company had a lot of success'? (mind éxito vs salida)
exercise.hint: éxito = success (NOT exit, which is salida); tener éxito = to be successful.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the verb: *encargarse de* (yo) — 'I'm in charge of the sales department.'
exercise.sentence: Yo me ___ del departamento de ventas.

accept: encargo

steps.title: Build: ask for a day off

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words: *I want to ask the boss for a day off.*
exercise.hint: pedir un día libre = ask for a day off; pedirle ... al jefe = ask the boss.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the preposition that goes with *asistir* (to attend): 'Tomorrow I attend an important meeting.'

accept: a

exercise.hint: asistir A algo = to attend something (asistir means attend, not assist).

steps.title: Carrera: a degree, not just a career

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I studied a law degree.* (use carrera for the university degree)
accept: Estudié la carrera de Derecho.
estudié la carrera de derecho
Estudie la carrera de Derecho.
Estudié una carrera de Derecho.
Estudié la carrera de Derecho
exercise.hint: carrera = university degree/major here; Derecho = Law.

exercise.prompt: Match each Spanish term with its English meaning.
Fill in the subjunctive of *pagar* (it/the company): 'I want a company that pays well.' (desired → subjunctive)

accept: pague

exercise.hint: Desired/unknown company → subjunctive; pagar → pague (spelling -gar to -gue).

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I want to apply for this position.* (use solicitar)
accept: Quiero solicitar este puesto.
quiero solicitar este puesto
Quiero solicitar este puesto
Quiero solicitar este puesto de trabajo.
exercise.hint: solicitar un puesto = to apply for a position.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I have experience in marketing and I'm responsible for the team.*
accept: Tengo experiencia en marketing y soy responsable del equipo.
tengo experiencia en marketing y soy responsable del equipo
Tengo experiencia en marketing y soy responsable del equipo
Tengo experiencia en marketing y soy responsable del equipo.
exercise.hint: tener experiencia en + field; ser responsable de + el equipo (del = de + el).
distractors: Tengo experiencia en marketing y estoy responsable del equipo.
Tengo éxito en marketing y soy responsable del equipo.
Asisto experiencia en marketing y soy responsable del equipo.
